channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: Negotiate remote pay without leaving home
item.description: Land $120k remote jobs from $5k markets — staging stack, latency proof, and salary negotiation scripts that turn geographic arbitrage into real paychecks.

item.title: Stop tuning max pool size
item.description: Set max pool size wrong and waste 40 % cloud spend — connection wait times jump 2× while databases sit idle. 2026 numbers and fixes for Node, Python, Go.

item.title: Show Africa not certs to land remote jobs
item.description: A production scar is any incident you’ve lived through and documented: a database melt-up at 03:17, a payment retry storm that cost $1,800 in duplicate…

item.title: Compare real-time: WebSockets, SSE, long polling
item.description: Choose WebSocket, SSE, or long polling in 2026 with hard latency, memory, and cost numbers from real load tests on Node 20 LTS, FastAPI 0.111, and Redis 7.2.

item.title: AI salary boosters: skills that paid in 2026
item.description: In 2026 only two AI skills pay reliably: prompt engineering for production systems and vector-database tuning. See the exact salary deltas, benchmarks, and tool stacks that move the needle.

item.title: Senior devs quit big tech—here’s why
item.description: Cut local iteration time from minutes to seconds — the hidden reasons senior engineers leave big tech and how to fix them with concrete metrics and code.

item.title: M-Pesa vs Paystack vs Flutterwave: which API bites?
item.description: Cut failed African payment integrations 70% with the right API choice — M-Pesa, Paystack, or Flutterwave benchmarks, fees, and hidden gotchas in 2026.

item.title: AI skills paying 40% more in 2026
item.description: AI skills paying 40% more in 2026: the data-backed breakdown of prompt engineering vs. AI engineering roles and what actually moves salary

item.title: Senior exits: Why FAANG burns out
item.description: Measure on-call load in 50 lines of Python — turn burnout into a metric you can negotiate with. 2026 attrition data from Glassdoor and Blind.

item.title: RAG pipelines: 3 things tutorials never tell you
item.description: The tutorials we read all promised 5-minute setups and latency under 500 ms at 95th percentile.

item.title: Why your DB pool size is probably wrong
item.description: Cut database connection pool waste 50% with real data — why the 2005 formula fails in 2026, SSL overhead cost, and the 3 settings most teams miss.

item.title: Portfolio hack: ship code, not templates
item.description: Portfolios that get remote jobs from Africa show production-like signals, not screenshots. Learn how to build a project that proves you can ship under load using Python 3.11, FastAPI, and Redis 7.2, with a $0 budget and 48-hour build time.

item.title: Benchmark WebSocket vs SSE vs long polling
item.description: Pick the right real-time tool in 90 seconds — latency benchmarks, memory costs, and the one AWS setting that breaks WebSockets

item.title: Land $120k remotely from $3k country
item.description: Land $120k remote roles from $3k monthly cost — the exact emails, contracts, and numbers that worked in 2026

item.title: Self-taught to senior in three years
item.description: Earn senior title without CS degree: ship real systems, break them, fix them. Learn Go, PostgreSQL, Redis and prove your worth with production scars.

item.title: RAG in production? Skip the toy demos
item.description: RAG in production fails when tutorials ignore metadata filters, IVFFlat indexing, and reranking — we cut latency 88% and costs 87% with Postgres + pgvector, FastAPI, and vLLM on T4 GPUs.

item.title: 2026 remote salary parity for LATAM devs
item.description: Generate your 2026 remote counter in 100 lines — parity engine, Mercer index, and the negotiation script top engineers use. Avoid the $75k anchor trap.

item.title: Senior devs quit for more than pay
item.description: Cut on-call pages 90%: data from 2026 shows senior devs flee big tech for autonomy, observability, and blameless culture — not just salary.

item.title: Skip CPU cores for DB pools
item.description: Cut database connection errors 80% by ignoring CPU cores—use max_connections and actual demand instead. Benchmarks, pitfalls, and the one setting every team configures wrong.

item.title: Show benchmarks, not GitHub stars
item.description: In my experience, this advice is only half right — and for 70% of African developers I’ve mentored, it’s actively harmful.
